intro: Mecardonia vandellioides, also known as Florida bluet, is a small, flowering annual plant native to the southeastern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Mecardonia vandellioides include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
